title: solanum multidissectum subsp. neohawkesii
intro: Solanum multidissectum subsp. neohawkesii, also known as the 'New Hawkes Bay nightshade', is a plant species within the Solanaceae family, native to New Zealand. It is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of solanine, a glycoalkaloid that has been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the management of conditions such as arthritis and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Solanum multidissectum subsp. neohawkesii are often used to create teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
